title: wheel-tender.sh Pass 2 stdin-drain bug: claude CLI in while loop drains here-string, only 1 spoke tended per nightly run
signal: Pass 2 of wheel-tender.sh uses 'while IFS= read -r SPOKE_PATH; do ... done <<< "$SPOKE_PATHS"'. The claude CLI invocation inside the loop had no stdin redirect, so it inherited the loop's here-string stdin and consumed remaining spoke paths on first iteration. Fix: add '< /dev/null' to the claude invocation. Bug caused only 1 spoke (framework, highest urgency) to receive teachings and work execution per nightly run. All other spokes were silently skipped.
rationale: This bug has been silently limiting the entire fleet tender to 1 spoke per night. With 17 spokes, 16 were never receiving teaching distribution or work execution from the Gardener. Fix is one line.
